sections.paragraph:
a root not used in Kal, prob. i.q. חָתַת, هَدَّ to break; hence to break in upon, to rush upon any one. Ἅπαξ λεγόμ.-

Poel. Psal. 62:4 , עַד אָנָה תְּהֹותְתוּ עַל אִישׁ “how long will ye rush upon a man?” LXX. ἐπιτίθεσθε. Vulg. irruitis. I do not agree in judgment with those who make the root הוּת, nor do I think the signification of making a noise (comp. Arab. هات) suitable to the passage.
